channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Trump administration drops lawsuit against company over alleged abuse at its child migrant shelters
item.description: McALLEN, Texas (AP) — The Trump administration moved to drop a civil lawsuit Wednesday against the largest provider of housing for migrant children over allegations of sexual abuse and harassment of unaccompanied minors, saying it also would no longer be using that company's services.
item.dc:creator: VALERIE GONZALEZ, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: 20 charged with murder after 3 die inside Nevada prison
item.description: Charges against the 20 defendants include first-degree murder and attempted murder with gang enhancements, prosecutors said.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, NewsNation

item.title: US toughens visitor registration rules for Canadians
item.description: The United States is cracking down on long-term visits from Canadians by now requiring visitors in the U.S. longer than 30 days to register with the government.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: One cat's incredible reunion with its owner after the LA wildfires
item.description: It appears the tall tale that all cats have nine lives may be true for a California Maine coon named Aggie. The beloved feline was feared dead for two months after the Palisades wildfire in Los Angeles left her family's home in ashes. But her owner, 82-year-old Katherine Kiefer, held out some hope.
item.dc:creator: BEATRICE DUPUY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: NTSB calls for ban on some helicopter routes near Reagan Airport after midair collision that killed 67 people
item.description: The National Transportation Safety Board is calling for a ban on some helicopter flights near Reagan Airport after an airliner and Army Black Hawk collided in January.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: NOAA set to fire 1,029 more employees
item.description: Staffing levels at the nation's already hobbled weather, climate and oceans agency could take a further nosedive in the days ahead.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Canada suspends 25% electricity tariffs on US
item.description: This comes after what Ford said was a "productive" conversation with U.S. Commerce Secretary Howard Lutnick.
item.dc:creator: Cassie Buchman, NewsNation

item.title: Sudiksha Konanki disappearance: Clothing of missing American student found on beach
item.description: Officials said missing University of Pittsburgh student Sudiksha Konanki's clothes were found on the beach in Punta Cana, Dominican Republic, where she disappeared.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 4 charged in death of 5-year-old boy 'incinerated' in hyperbaric chamber explosion
item.description: The boy, Thomas Cooper, was “incinerated” inside a pressurized oxygen chamber that exploded at Oxford Center, a medical facility in a Detroit suburb, on Jan. 31. His mother was standing next to the chamber and was injured trying to save her boy.
item.dc:creator: Daylyn Huff, KRON4

item.title: House GOP tees up final vote on bill to avert shutdown
item.description: House Republicans on Tuesday advanced their bill to avert an end-of-the-week government shutdown, teeing up a final vote on the legislation later in the day. The chamber voted 216-214 to adopt a rule — which governs debate on legislation — for Speaker Mike Johnson’s (R-La.) continuing resolution.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, NewsNation

item.title: Texas man 'mad' at Trump arrested, accused of assaulting fellow passenger on flight to DC
item.description: Man who allegedly committed assault aboard flight to DCA indicated that he had traveled to D.C. because he wanted to speak to President Donald Trump, an affidavit indicates.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Former US Rep. Katie Porter steps into crowded California governor's race
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Former Democratic U.S. House member Katie Porter announced Tuesday that she is entering the 2026 contest for California governor, joining a crowded field of candidates that could be upended if former Vice President Kamala Harris joins the race.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. BLOOD, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Why you'll no longer see 'excessive heat warnings' in the forecast
item.description: It may be hard to think about sweltering temperatures in early March but excessive heat watches and warnings are getting a new name.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, WFLA, NBC

item.title: 15 injured, 1 seriously, when school bus overturns in New Jersey: officials
item.description: At least 14 juveniles were injured when a school bus overturned on the Garden State Parkway in New Jersey on Monday, officials said.
item.dc:creator: Ben Mitchell, Fox 5

item.title: Bags fly free no more: Southwest Airlines to end free checked luggage policy
item.description: Southwest Airlines will no longer allow bags to fly free. The airline announced Tuesday it will begin charging some customers who check baggage in May.
item.dc:creator: Christopher Adams, ABC 4

item.title: 3 killed after medical helicopter crashes in Mississippi
item.description: Three people have died after a medical helicopter crashed near the Natchez Trace in Madison County, Mississippi.
item.dc:creator: Kaitlin Howell, Fox 5

item.title: Education Department says 60 universities, including Northwestern University, under investigation for antisemitism
item.description: The Department of Education’s Office of Civil Rights (OCR) is investigating 60 universities for antisemitism, according to letters sent on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, WGN 9

item.title: Computer owners duped in antivirus scam to get over $25M: What to know about payments
item.description: More than $25 million in payments will go out this week to customers who were tricked into paying for fraudulent tech support offered by two companies.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, KRON4

item.title: Social Security to again recoup 100% of overpayments
item.description: The Office of the Chief Actuary estimated the withholding rate increase could save about $7 billion over the next decade.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, Fox 5

item.title: Musk eyes Social Security and benefit programs for cuts while claiming widespread fraud
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Elon Musk pushed debunked theories about Social Security on Monday while describing federal benefit programs as rife with fraud, suggesting they will be a primary target in his crusade to reduce government spending.
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: PHOTOS: 132-year-old wreck of 'one of the safest ships afloat' discovered in Lake Superior
item.description: The Great Lakes Shipwreck Historical Society has announced the discovery of a new wreck on Lake Superior: The SS Western Reserve.
item.dc:creator: Matt Jaworowski, WGN 9

item.title: Statements man charged in Atlanta spa killings made post-arrest can be used at trial, judge rules
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — A judge on Monday rejected an attempt by attorneys for a man accused of killing four women at Atlanta massage businesses to keep a jury from hearing statements he made after his arrest.
item.dc:creator: KATE BRUMBACK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Juarez police officer injured in body shop attack that left 3 dead
item.description: EL PASO, Texas (Border Report) – Three men were killed and two injured by gunfire during a shootout Sunday at a mechanical shop in Juarez, Mexico. Juarez police said at least two men arrived in a vehicle at the business in the Heroes de la Revolucion neighborhood around 7 p.m.
item.dc:creator: Julian Resendiz, Fox 5

item.title: Trump DOT chief rejects inclusion of environmental justice factors in infrastructure decisions
item.description: Transportation Secretary Sean Duffy on Monday rescinded Biden-era memos that he said called on state agencies to factor a “social justice and environmental agenda” into infrastructure project decisions.
item.dc:creator: ALEXA ST. JOHN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: US added to human rights watchlist over Trump moves
item.description: The United States has been added to CIVICUS's monitor list of potential human rights threats due to President Trump's restrictive executive orders and institutional cutbacks, which are creating an atmosphere to chill democratic dissent.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Crisp, Fox 5

item.title: Stroller accessory recalled after 6 children suffer finger lacerations: CPSC
item.description: Consumers who may have purchased or installed the accessories are being instructed to immediately discontinue use.
item.dc:creator: Talia Naquin, WFLA, NBC

item.title: EPA froze 'green bank' funds worth billions, climate group suit says
item.description: A nonprofit that was awarded nearly $7 billion by the Biden administration to finance clean energy and climate-friendly projects has sued the Environmental Protection Agency, accusing it of improperly freezing its account.
item.dc:creator: Michael Phillis And Matthew Daly, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Young people who aspired to government service dismayed by Trump ending the federal fellows program
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A young economist who had uprooted her life for civil service. A fierce housing advocate terminated just before buying her first home. A semifinalist whose dreams were dashed before they materialized.
item.dc:creator: GARY FIELDS and CHRISTINE FERNANDO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: President Trump defends tariff policy
item.description: President Donald Trump continues to defend tariffs as a way to address unfair trade and illegal drugs coming across the border, but critics warn they'll cause higher prices for Americans.
item.dc:creator: Maddie Biertempfel, WGN 9

item.title: Measles case confirmed in Maryland resident who recently travelled abroad
item.description: Early symptoms include a fever higher than 101 degrees, a runny nose, cough and red, watery eyes. A person will experience a red rash one to four days after early symptoms.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, Fox 5

item.title: What to know about Mark Carney, elected next Canadian prime minister
item.description: Mark Carney, a former central banker, won his Liberal Party's election in a landslide on Sunday to replace Justin Trudeau as prime minister of Canada. He will be sworn into office in the coming days.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, Fox 5

item.title: AI made its way to vineyards. Here's how the technology is helping make your wine
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — When artificial intelligence-backed tractors became available to vineyards, Tom Gamble wanted to be an early adopter. He knew there would be a learning curve, but Gamble decided the technology was worth figuring out. The third-generation farmer bought one autonomous tractor.
item.dc:creator: SARAH PARVINI, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Cavaliers rely on scoring balance to beat Bucks 112-100 for 14th consecutive victory
item.description: MILWAUKEE (AP) — Max Strus had 17 points to lead a balanced scoring attack as the NBA-leading Cleveland Cavaliers defeated the Milwaukee Bucks 112-100 on Sunday night for their 14th consecutive victory.
item.dc:creator: STEVE MEGARGEE, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Trump pushes to shut down the Dept. of Education: What impact it has on Florida
item.description: Florida relies on billions in Federal education funding, from special education to student loan programs.
item.dc:creator: Georgia McCarthur, WFLA, NBC

item.title: South Carolina man called for end of death penalty before execution in final statement
item.description: "I want my closing statement to be one of love and a calling to my fellow Christians to help us end the death penalty," Brad Kieth Sigmon wrote ahead of his execution.
item.dc:creator: Kelci O'Donnell, ABC 4

item.title: Ex-central banker to replace Trudeau as Canada's prime minister after winning Liberal Party vote
item.description: Former central banker Mark Carney will become Canada’s next prime minister after the governing Liberal Party elected him its leader Sunday as the country deals with U.S. President Donald Trump’s trade war and annexation threat, and a federal election looms.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Top US health agency makes $25,000 buyout offer to most of its 80,000 employees
item.description: The mass email went out to a “broad population of HHS employees," landing in their inboxes days before agency heads are due to offer plans for shrinking their workforces.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9
